name: Pumpkin Spice Cake in Jars
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F.
2. Sterilize 8 (1 pint, straight-sided) Kerr or Ball Quilted Crystal (12 oz, straight-sided, Ball #14400-81400) canning jars, lids, and rings by boiling them in water for 10 minutes. Remove jars from water and allow to air-dry. Keep lids and rings in hot water until ready to use. Once cool, brush the inside of jars with shortening (do not use Pam or Baker's Secret); set aside.
3. Coarsely chop nuts and raisins; set aside.
4. Sift together flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, cloves, cinnamon, and ginger in a large bowl. Add raisins and walnuts; toss lightly to combine.
5. In another large bowl, beat eggs at high speed until thick and yellow (2-3 minutes). Gradually beat in sugar until thick and light. Beat in oil and pumpkin slowly; blend thoroughly.
6. Gradually stir flour mixture into the egg mixture until well blended.
7. Divide batter among the 8 canning jars (should be slightly less than half full). Wipe sides of jars inside/outside if needed to avoid slops or burning. Place jars on a cookie sheet so they don't tip over. Bake in preheated oven at 325°F for 35-40 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
8. When cakes are done, remove jars one-by-one, place lids and rings on each jar, and screw tightly. Use heavy-duty mitts when handling hot jars. Place jars on counter to cool. Sealed jars will make a "plinking" sound. If unsure, press lids once jars are cooled; they should not move. Store in a cool, dark place for up to 1 year.
